extraction.one_line_summary: GILBERT Philippe won from 2-man breakaway sprint against Politt in Roubaix velodrome, finishing seconds ahead of closing Lampaert at 40 seconds.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT Wout crashed twice after chasing back from mechanicals then entered breakaway fully spent, showing futile energy expenditure.
SAGAN Peter's teammate OSS Daniel crashed early, forcing defending champion into 25-second chase that neutralized his defense hopes.
GILBERT drove early breakaway pace deliberately to drop teammate KÜNG before Sagan's arrival, preventing numerical disadvantage in finale.
LAMPAERT Yves closed 40-second gap to 18 seconds during velodrome sprint, proving late chasers nearly catch tactical hesitation.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: Double crashes after mechanicals consumed energy bridging to winning break, left him unable to contest finale despite reaching it
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Loss of key domestique OSS to crash forced him into costly 25-second chase, ending back-to-back defense attempt
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: Tactically ejected teammate KÜNG from break before Sagan arrived, then held sprint positioning discipline against bigger Politt
rider_signals.POLITT Nils: neutral: Physical size disadvantage ('big rider, puffing') in velodrome banking prevented him from going over Gilbert on track
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: positive: Solo chase reduced 40-second deficit to 18 seconds during front duo's velodrome hesitation, nearly caught them
